summary: Comprehensive testing of Admin Login Intelligence module (backend + frontend). 18/18 backend pytest assertions passed (admin auto-promotion, /admin/me 200/401/403 gating, /admin/login-events pagination + filter by email/event_type/login_method, NO raw ip_address ever returned, ip_address_hash is 24-char hex, summary endpoint shape, event recording on register/login_failed/logout, UA parsing for iPhone/Chrome/Android, cf-ipcountry geo extraction). 11/11 frontend Playwright checks effectively passed: logged-out redirects to /login (visually confirmed via screenshot — wait_for_url matched), non-admin sees admin-forbidden 403 card, admin sees full page with all 6 summary cards + filter block + 34 event rows, Admin nav link gated correctly (hidden for non-admin, visible for admin), email filter narrows results correctly (34 → 11 sr-tester-only rows), no horizontal overflow at 1440x900 or 375x667, network-level confirmation that /api/admin/login-events response contains 'ip_address_hash' but NOT 'ip_address'.
critical_code_review_comments: logout endpoint reads session_token from Cookie only (not Bearer header) — if a client only sends Bearer, the logout event won't be recorded and the session won't be deleted. Consider also accepting Authorization header in /api/auth/logout for parity with get_current_user. Not blocking (cookie+localStorage both set on login) but worth noting.

_BROWSER_PATTERNS uses 'first-match wins' iteration where Edge is checked before Chrome. Good. However Safari is checked before Firefox/Opera; Chrome UA strings contain 'Safari/' too — but Chrome is matched first so OK. Firefox UA does NOT contain Safari/ so safe. Logic is correct.
admin.py is 305 lines — fine, under the 700-line guideline.
Privacy posture is clean: _strip_event explicitly drops _id and ip_address keys before returning, and find() projects {_id: 0}. Good defense in depth.
test_credentials: Admin: sr-tester@example.com / TestPass123! (auto-promoted via ADMIN_EMAILS env). Non-admin: any newly-registered user.
seed_data_creation: Tests created multiple TEST_-prefixed user accounts during run (TEST_nonadmin_*, TEST_reg_*, TEST_lo_*, TEST_unk_*, TEST_iphone_*, TEST_chr_*, TEST_and_*, TEST_geo_*, TEST_geo_pub_*, TEST_uifn_*). They are isolated by email prefix and safe to clean up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin Login Intelligence is fully verified. Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_admin_login_intelligence.py — reuse for regression. Note: in this preview env, public ingress strips inbound 'cf-ipcountry' header so events posted via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL get ip_country=null. The geo-extraction logic is correct and is verified by hitting localhost:8001 directly (test_cf_ipcountry_header_via_localhost). This is documented behavior, not a bug.
